title: Fix EXIF stripping bug — stop lying in PhotoUploadResponse
queue_body:

Replace stub StripExifMetadata (PhotoUploadEndpoints.cs:241-248) with real Magick.NET EXIF/IPTC/XMP/GPS scrub. Tie ExifStripped flag to strip result, not file type. Reject uploads on strip failure (422).

## User decision 2026-04-20 (tightened DoD)
- Implement via Magick.NET Image.Strip(); handles JPEG/PNG/HEIC
- ExifStripped = stripResult.Success; never tied to !isPdf
- Strip failure → 422, no persistence
- Integration test with committed EXIF-laden fixture
- Architecture test: no pipeline accepts raw image bytes, only StripResult.Scrubbed

## Files
- src/shared/Cena.Infrastructure/Media/ExifStripper.cs (new)
- src/api/Cena.Student.Api.Host/Endpoints/PhotoUploadEndpoints.cs (replace stub + rewire flag + 422 path)

title: Implement crypto-shredding for misconception events (pending ADR-003a)
queue_body:
## Goal
Implement the erasure model selected by prr-003a (assumed: crypto-shredding Option A). Replace read-filter stub at RetentionWorker.cs:334-357.

## Components
1. Per-subject key store (separate from Marten; PostgreSQL table)
2. EncryptedFieldAccessor seam (write-encrypt, read-try-decrypt with [erased] sentinel)

title: Sandbox SymPy template evaluation in problem-variation engine

## Goal
Sandbox SymPy template evaluation at three layers (container, SymPy whitelist, canary tests). Current sidecar has only ms-timeout (SymPySidecarClient.cs:103) — insufficient for mem blow-up, recursion, escape. LLM templates are untrusted input.

title: Redesign 'At-Risk Student Alert' — honest + supportive + legal
queue_body:
## Goal
Redesign under three hard constraints (session-scope, in-surface-only, confidence-bound) using honest reality-based language, not soft euphemism. User decision 2026-04-20: harsh-reality numbers are the target voice; euphemism is the anti-pattern.

## Hard constraints (not tone-negotiable)
1. Session-scoped only (ADR-0003) — never persisted to profile/aggregate.
2. In-surface only (RDY-080) — never emitted to parent SMS, gradebook passback, or dashboards loading after session.
3. Confidence-bounded — every number carries CI + sample size.

## Language policy (user-directed)
- Target: 'You answered 40% of mastery-threshold problems correctly today — that's below the 5-unit trajectory.'
- Banned euphemism: 'needs support', 'room to grow', 'opportunity area', 'at risk', 'concerning'.
- Ship-gate scanner enforces.

## Files
- docs/adr/NNNN-at-risk-session-surface.md
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Sessions/SessionRiskAssessment.cs (new VO: point+CI+sampleSize+generatedAt)
- tests/arch/NoAtRiskPersistenceTest.cs
- tests/arch/NoAtRiskExternalEmissionTest.cs
- src/student/full-version/src/components/session/TrajectoryIndicator.vue
- src/admin/full-version/src/views/apps/session/TeacherSessionView.vue
- AXIS_5/AXIS_6/competitive doc updates

## Definition of Done
- ADR accepted. SessionRiskAssessment lives inside session actor, absent from StudentState and any snapshot.
- Two arch tests green: no risk* field on persisted/external DTOs; no outbound adapter emits risk.
- UI renders number + CI; e2e asserts banned-euphemism list absent from trajectory component.

title: ADR: BKT fixed-parameter policy + worked-example fading hysteresis
queue_body:
## Goal
Author docs/adr/NNNN-bkt-parameters-and-fading.md — policy ADR binding concrete BKT values + worked-example fading hysteresis + cohort difficulty targets.

## User decision 2026-04-20 (concrete values adopted)
- BKT parameters (Koedinger lab defaults, locked): pInit=0.3, pLearn=0.15, pSlip=0.10, pGuess=0.15
- No per-student parameter learning (ADR-0003 extension — any tuning requires new ADR + human sign-off)
- Worked-example fading hysteresis: 3-consecutive-correct to fade L→L-1; any L-1 failure restores to L; min 2 attempts per level
- Difficulty target: 75% default (not 60% — demoralizing per persona-educator); 85% pre-exam (30-day window); anxious profile +5pp
- Architecture test BktParametersLockedTest: no code path overrides the four constants

## Files
- docs/adr/NNNN-bkt-parameters-and-fading.md
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Mastery/BktParameters.cs (new — single source of truth)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Mastery/BktService.cs (consume BktParameters)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Mastery/ScaffoldingService.cs (hysteresis impl)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Mastery/DifficultyTarget.cs
- tests/architecture/BktParametersLockedTest.cs
- tests/integration/ScaffoldingHysteresisTests.cs

## Definition of Done
1. ADR accepted; cited from BktParameters.cs file header
2. Four constants in BktParameters.cs; BktService consumes exclusively
3. Hysteresis wired; unit + integration tests green
4. DifficultyTarget: 75/85 + motivation-profile +5pp
5. BktParametersLockedTest green
6. No 60% target remains anywhere (search + confirm)
7. Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly

## Rolls up into EPIC-PRR-A
Sequence: after prr-002 (done 2026-04-20), before ScaffoldingService-touching features.

title: ADR: Accommodation profile scope (student vs enrollment)
queue_body:
## Goal
ADR for Accommodations aggregate scope + Ministry-parity claim. Accommodations are durable profile settings (NOT ADR-0003 session-scoped — Ministry lens confirmed).

## Ministry lens 2026-04-20 (persona-ministry YAML read)
- Durable, audit-logged, mirrors Ministry diagnoses
- Parity-claim required: Cena runtime accommodations ⊇ Ministry Bagrut exam accommodations
- F7 Arabic RTL math is shipping precondition (non-negotiable #6 already)
- F6 MathML pipeline understated — must match Bagrut framework

## User decision 2026-04-20 (adopt hybrid + mandatory sections)
- Student-profile-scoped: core disability-backed accommodations (dyscalculia/dyslexia/ADHD/visual/hearing/motor) — follow institute transfers
- Enrollment-scoped: institute-specific variants (seating, extra-time variant, assistive-tech preferences)

## ADR mandatory sections
1. Scope decision + rationale (hybrid)
2. Parity-claim matrix (Ministry ↔ Cena, per row)
3. Storage distinction (durable aggregate vs session-scoped misconception) + arch test
4. Audit log requirement per change, authorization source
5. Cross-tenant transfer semantics (ADR-0001 Phase 2+)
6. Diagnosis-certificate attachment protocol or "pending MoE clarification"

## Files
- docs/adr/NNNN-accommodation-scope-and-bagrut-parity.md
- docs/compliance/bagrut-accommodations-parity-matrix.md
- Cross-ref updates: ADR-0001 + ADR-0003
- tests/architecture/AccommodationStorageDistinctionTest.cs

## Definition of Done
1. ADR accepted with all 6 sections
2. Parity matrix signed off by human-architect + Ministry-lens reviewer
3. Storage-distinction arch test green
4. Cross-references updated
5. Diagnosis-certificate protocol documented or explicitly deferred
6. Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly

title: Design ConsentAggregate + events
queue_body:
## Goal
Design ConsentAggregate + events as new bounded context, replacing scattered consent state on StudentActor/GdprConsentManager. EPIC-PRR-C event-schema review required before commit.

## User decision 2026-04-20 — design sketch + blocked-by + cross-review gate

## Blocked-by (hard)
- prr-003a (erasure model — consent events carry PII; encryption seam must be known)
- prr-014 (age-band rules — consent events carry ActorRole and authorization invariants)

## Event schema sketch (4 minimum)
- ConsentGranted_V1 (subjectId, purpose, scope, grantedByRole, grantedByActorId, timestamp, expiresAt)
- ConsentRevoked_V1 (subjectId, purpose, revokedByRole, revokedByActorId, timestamp, reason)
- ConsentPurposeAdded_V1 (consentId, newPurpose, addedByRole, timestamp)
- ConsentReviewedByParent_V1 (studentSubjectId, parentActorId, purposesReviewed, outcome, timestamp)

## Stream key
consent-{subjectId} — subject is student OR parent (independent streams)

## GdprConsentManager relation
New primitive is ConsentAggregate; GdprConsentManager refactored to thin read-side facade that projects events into existing DTO shape. Preserves API contract.

## Age-band interaction
Events carry ActorRole; aggregate command handler enforces age-band authorization invariants from prr-014 ADR. 14-year-old cannot grant purposes requiring parent.

## Cross-review gate
EPIC-PRR-C owner must sign off event schema via PR comment before merge.

## Files
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Consent/ConsentAggregate.cs (new)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Consent/Events/ (4 events)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Consent/ConsentCommands.cs
- src/shared/Cena.Infrastructure/Compliance/GdprConsentManager.cs (refactor to facade)
- docs/adr/NNNN-consent-aggregate.md
- tests/unit/Consent/ + tests/integration/Consent/
- tests/architecture/ConsentAggregateNoProfileCouplingTest.cs

## Definition of Done
1. prr-003a accepted (blocker)
2. prr-014 accepted (blocker)
3. ConsentAggregate + 4 events implemented; command handlers enforce age-band invariants
4. GdprConsentManager refactored to facade; DTO API unchanged
5. PII fields wired through EncryptedFieldAccessor per prr-003a
6. EPIC-PRR-C owner PR sign-off recorded in merge commit
7. No consent state on StudentActor/StudentProfile post-merge (arch test)
8. Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly; tests pass
9. Micro-ADR cross-references prr-003a + prr-014
